keyPoints: Automatically learns hierarchical features from raw data, eliminating the need for manual feature engineering.
Excels at handling unstructured data like images, text, audio, and video.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance on complex tasks like image recognition, machine translation, and speech recognition.
Scales effectively with large amounts of data and computational power, continuously improving.
commonMistakes: Thinking DL is always the best solution; for simpler, structured data problems, traditional ML is often more efficient and interpretable.
Underestimating the computational resources (hardware, time, and cost) required to train deep learning models effectively.
Believing DL models are 'magic'; they still require high-quality data, careful tuning, and a clear problem definition to work well.
